name: Stuffed Fish Fillets
recipeIngredient: 4 Red snapper or flounder fillets (5 oz each)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F. In a 10-inch skillet, melt the margarine; remove 1 tablespoon and set aside. Heat the remaining margarine until bubbly and hot; add the scallions and green peppers to the skillet and sauté over medium heat until the vegetables are softened, about 3 minutes. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
2. Add the bread crumbs, beaten egg, 1/4 cup chopped parsley, salt, nutmeg, and red pepper flakes to the vegetable mixture and mix well until all ingredients are moistened. Spoon 1/4 of the vegetable mixture over each fillet and roll the fish to enclose it. Transfer the stuffed fillets, seam side down, to an 8 x 8 x 2 baking pan, spooning any remaining filling over them. Drizzle the reserved margarine over the fillets and sprinkle each with 1/2 tablespoon lemon juice.
3. Bake until the fish flakes easily when tested with a fork, about 15-20 minutes. Sprinkle with remaining parsley before serving.
